codeReview.summary: The settlement module covers the requested F5 moneyline and run-line grading rules, returns the expected WIN/LOSS/PUSH/NO_ACTION statuses, settles only the targeted game_pk for doubleheaders, and persists settlement effects through the existing bankroll updater into both bets and bankroll_ledger. I did not find code-level correctness issues in the reviewed feature diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to discover a Windows-specific startup fallback even though this repo already exposes Windows-safe init commands; the base worker startup procedure should explicitly direct PowerShell sessions to use the services manifest instead of attempting bash-only init.sh execution first.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session 36ae509a-d7ca-4964-859a-886e3c605c40 shows two failed bash invocations of .factory/init.sh before switching approaches, and handoff.skillFeedback.deviations records that the worker fell back to .factory/services.yaml init steps on Windows. The repo already documents the Windows-safe path in .factory/services.yaml (commands.init_windows) and .factory/library/environment.md (Windows shell gotcha).

summary: Pass. The reviewed t23 settlement implementation matches the feature contract and adds adequate test coverage for ML/RL grading, no-action paths, bankroll persistence, and game_pk-based doubleheader handling. I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues in the feature diff; the only follow-up is a shared-state skill note about Windows-safe initialization guidance.
